title: Cloud Deployment Models

sections.title: Types of Cloud Deployments
sections.content: Organizations can deploy cloud computing in different ways based on their needs. The four main deployment models are: Public Cloud, Private Cloud, Hybrid Cloud, and Multi-Cloud. Each model offers different benefits in terms of cost, control, and flexibility.

sections.title: Public Cloud
sections.content:
**What is Public Cloud?**
Cloud services offered by third-party providers over the public internet. Resources are shared among multiple customers ('multi-tenant') but data is kept separate and secure.

**Characteristics:**
- Owned and operated by cloud provider (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
- Shared infrastructure across many customers
- Pay-as-you-go pricing
- No upfront costs
- Fully managed by provider
cons: May not meet strict compliance requirements

sections.title: Private Cloud
sections.content:
**What is Private Cloud?**
Cloud infrastructure dedicated exclusively to one organization. Can be hosted on-premises (your data center) or by a third-party provider, but resources are not shared with other organizations.

**Characteristics:**
- Dedicated infrastructure for single organization
- Can be on-premises or hosted
- More control and customization
- Higher costs than public cloud
- Organization responsible for management
examples: AWS Outposts (AWS in your data center)

sections.title: Hybrid Cloud
sections.content:
**What is Hybrid Cloud?**
Combination of public and private clouds that work together. Allows data and applications to move between the two environments. Offers 'best of both worlds' flexibility.

**Characteristics:**
- Mix of on-premises and public cloud
- Data and applications can move between environments
- Orchestration between platforms
- Balanced control and scalability
examples: Critical data on-premises, web apps on public cloud
bestFor: Keeping sensitive data on-premises while using cloud for processing
pros: Flexibility - choose where to run workloads

sections.title: Multi-Cloud
sections.content:
**What is Multi-Cloud?**
Using multiple public cloud providers (AWS + Azure + Google Cloud) for different services. Different from hybrid cloud - all providers are public clouds, no private cloud component.

**Characteristics:**
- Multiple cloud providers simultaneously
- Different services from different providers
- Avoid vendor lock-in
- Use best service from each provider
examples: AWS for compute + Google Cloud for AI/ML
Azure for Microsoft workloads + AWS for storage
Distribute workloads across AWS, Azure, and GCP
pros: Use best service from each provider

advantages: Public Cloud - Best for cost and scalability
Private Cloud - Best for control and compliance
Hybrid Cloud - Best for gradual migration
Multi-Cloud - Best for avoiding vendor lock-in
Choose based on your security, cost, and control needs
